rules: Every image/audio asset needs source, author, license, and commercial-use status before release.
Unknown or placeholder assets must be replaced or formally approved.
Every gameplay animation cycle must have a generated contact sheet before it is approved.

stage-01-bg.author: unknown
stage-01-bg.notes: Active Stage 1 scene plate because it meets the current visual quality target better than prototype tiles. Gameplay still comes from Tiled-style object layers. Source/license must be documented or replaced before release.

metro-tiles.author: project prototype
metro-tiles.notes: Prototype/editor helper tileset only. It must not be used as the primary visible stage art until replaced with production-quality tiles/prefabs.

player-sheet.author: unknown
player-sheet.notes: Manual frame rectangles are defined in /data/animations.json. Requires contact-sheet approval for every cycle.

enemy-sheet.author: unknown
enemy-sheet.notes: Walk cycle was manually replaced during prototype work. Requires ongoing contact-sheet review.

striker-sheet.source: generated from local prototype enemy-rival-sheet via tools/generate-enemy-role-sheets.mjs
striker-sheet.author: project prototype pipeline
striker-sheet.commercialUse: blocked until replaced or formally approved
striker-sheet.notes: Runtime role-specific texture. This improves role identity but is not final hand-authored enemy art.

switchblade-sora-sheet.source: Cleaned and cropped from project-provided high-resolution generated sheet public/assets/sprites/bosses/incoming/switchblade-sora-white-bg.png via tools/build-switchblade-sora-sheet.py
switchblade-sora-sheet.author: project-generated asset provided by Moncef Benboubakeur
switchblade-sora-sheet.commercialUse: blocked until contact-sheet review and project approval
switchblade-sora-sheet.notes: Unique runtime sheet for Switchblade Sora. Contact sheets live under docs/contact-sheets/bosses/switchblade-sora. Not production-approved until human visual review and licensing approval are complete.

turnstile-ren-sheet.notes: Separate runtime boss texture. This removes Phaser runtime tinting but is not final unique boss art.
